senses.gloss: to strike, to knock, to hack, to dig into, to tap, to peck; literally
to strike, to knock, to hack, to dig into, to tap, to peck; so as investigating, to dig into, to tap
to strike, to knock, to hack, to dig into, to tap, to peck; so as making a certain sound, to click (also on the computer), to knock, to thump, pluck (a drum as well as a string instrument)

examples.text:
جِلَّوْز عَيْن الثَّوْر
شَيْئًا مَلْهَوِيّ
يَنْقُر لَك فَالْبَاب
نَقْرً مُسْتَوِيّ
يَصَّدَّع رَأْسَك
فَذَاك الدَوِيّ
وَرِزْق الْجِلَّوْز
فَذَاك الصُّدَاع
examples.english:
Filberts and prunes
are frolic forsooth!
Your door is pinged
to have them slinged,
stopless din and ballyhoo!
Each cobnut wreaks you grue!

senses.gloss: to strike, to knock, to hack, to dig into, to tap, to peck; so as offending a person, to injure, to bully
to dig into, to peck, to stipple, to pit

etymology.text: Compare Latin fodere for semantical development. Cognate to the Hebrew root ניקר / נִקֵּר (nikér, “to bore, gouge out”)
